contact_lookup_status: No named recruiter was visible on the live StepStone posting checked on 2026-03-28. The posting refers applicants to HR Services via myhrservice@mercedes-benz.com and +49 711 17 99000, so the letter uses HR Services as the fallback.
job_title: Working Student Product Owner Support Insurance Platform of Services
why_company: Mercedes-Benz Insurance Services stands out to me because this role connects product support, requirements, testing, data-driven insights, and agile delivery in a structured digital platform environment.
job_description: Mercedes-Benz Insurance Services is hiring a working student to support Product Owners and Requirements Engineers on the Insurance Platform of Services. The role includes gathering and structuring business requirements, facilitating discussions with development teams, supporting test planning and automated testing, helping agile planning processes, collecting product data and user feedback for actionable insights, preparing management presentations, and supporting product communication and internal updates.
requirements: Studies in computer science, business informatics, or a related field with at least three semesters
Prior experience as a working student or intern is highly desirable
Business fluent in English; German is advantageous
High degree of self-motivation and initiative
Strong digital affinity and interest in technology
Analytical mindset and attention to detail
Interest in agile product work, testing, and product insights
cv_about_override: Product owner and business analysis professional focused on digital platforms, requirements, testing support, and data-informed product improvement.
cv_summary_override: Experienced product and business analysis professional with a strong foundation in research, requirements, stakeholder alignment, rollout, and continuous improvement. I have worked across eCommerce, LegalTech, healthcare, and digital operations, and I am particularly strong in structured execution, cross-functional coordination, and turning user and business needs into practical product decisions. This makes me well suited for working student roles that combine product support, agile collaboration, testing, and data-driven improvement.
cover_letter_opening: I am applying for the position of Working Student Product Owner Support Insurance Platform of Services at Mercedes-Benz Insurance Services GmbH. This role stands out to me because it combines requirements work, testing support, agile collaboration, and product insights in a structured digital platform environment.
cover_letter_body_one: In my previous roles across digital products and operations-heavy environments, I worked closely with stakeholders, designers, engineers, and business teams to structure requirements, improve workflows, and support execution. That experience gave me a strong foundation in turning business needs into practical product decisions and keeping teams aligned around clear delivery goals.
cover_letter_body_two: I am particularly comfortable in roles that connect product support with structured analysis. Through my work across marketplace products, regulated onboarding, and healthcare workflow digitization, I have built experience in documenting requirements, supporting delivery, coordinating across functions, and using feedback and data to improve product decisions. This is closely aligned with the mix of Product Owner support, testing, insights, and communication described in your posting.
cover_letter_motivation: I am especially interested in this role because the Insurance Platform of Services sits at the intersection of digital product development, customer experience, and data-driven improvement. It is exactly the kind of environment where I can contribute while continuing to grow toward stronger product ownership.
cover_letter_closing: I am available to start from 01 May 2026 and can work up to 20 hours per week in line with my current student visa. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how I can support the Insurance Platform of Services team in an interview.
